item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not gonna lie, I find this kinda funny, but it's tales about killing humans kinda unerved me. Still, it's more funny than threatening.

item.title: A giant space whale.
item.content:encoded: Interesting idea, also it's a whale in space so yeah. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would read this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Reminds me of Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Markiplier's off playthrough. Enough said.

SPACE WHALE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This whole thing is an aesthetic I am very much a fan of. I feel like there's a lot of potential for there to be maybe something more? But as it is, I really love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The immortal soviet cosmonaut would ride the desiccated space whale off into the stars for space-opera-style adventures.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What do you think would happen if SCP-2146 and SCP-1959 collided?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As I was reading this, up until the point I started reading the transmission logs, I thought it was faintly ridiculous. After I read them, it seemed mildly creepy. After thinking a bit, it seems like it's
+ show block
– hide block

a dead whale in space that transmits innocuous, if disturbing audio segments. How it got there is a complete mystery, as is why and how it transmits anything. It's wierd, ridiculous, and somewhat funny for some reason.

You have earned a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Here's your 100th upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Space whale is nearing +100 upvotes! Very nice!

Also, stay tuned for a possible broadcast recording of one of these! Really think it'd be fun!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a thought for the "punching up" piece: how about a broadcast that castigates the rest of the whales on Earth for a) not buying into its "let go to space and become planet destroyers" plan or b) settling down on the planet they were going to destroy. You'd need to decide on whether they were actually space whales to begin with, or if that was just something it made up. Or you could add both at different points and leave it unclear, but with a bit more pathos than is currently there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
'And the children all died. They should have paid more attention to the lesson, I guess!'

goddammit +1

item.title: Nice Reference
item.content:encoded: This looks like a reference to a "Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy" reference so +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A space whale containing an interstellar Jonah who pushes his own space whale ideology on Earthlings. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They should have paid more attention to the lesson, I guess!'

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You may have not meant to imply that the whale is extraterrestrial, but the article has taken a life of its own (despite being about a corpse) and is very much implying it.

I would suggest to reassess the situation. Sit back, have a drink, and read through it. Look at what the article is trying to become, not what you originally intended it to be, and help it become that thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Whoops, fixed. Thanks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The sheer bizzarity (I'm sure it's a word) of this SCP is just somehow… pleasing. It seems quite a few other members would agree, judging by the rating - and it's your first, too, which I'm basically going to worship you for. Nicely done! +1

One little gripe, though. Please, please, please fix this:

They should of paid more attention to the lesson, I guess!

My inner Grammar Nazi is screaming in inarticulate German.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea that there are space whales out there and they want to destroy us. I like how all the broadcasts are like thinly desguised threats that whales from space are gonna $#@! our !@#$ up. I don't know if a mafia style "it would be a shame if anything were to happen" was what your were going for, but that's what I got from this and I liked it. +1 for now.
I

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's no mention of the GOC at all here. There's no reason to suppose that these elements exist concurrently.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only thing I dislike about this SCP is this footnote:

This information was falsified and entered into the UN Register of Objects Launched into Space by imbedded agents. It is believed such a registration will discourage close scrutiny.

The reason is, following the GOC canon, the UN should have the means to link the Standard Communications Production to the Foundation (by tracing the initials). With this in mind, the UN/GOC would actually encourage scrutiny on this particular object to the point where they could find out what it is and make a Gundam Orange Suit magically appear next to it and destroy the object…

Other than that +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1 for space whales. The whole "weird broadcasts" thing is a little overdone, but the article as a whole is charming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's something about this that clicks with me. Maybe it's the fact that the Foundation seems to be pretty blase about the prospect of Earth being eaten by space whales, or that the space whales announce it with cheap melodrama and silly children's programming. Maybe they think it can't be a serious threat to Earth because of the ridiculousness of it, or they think there's no reason to assume it's a credible threat. Maybe a secondary memetic effect? At least that's a possibility.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, as I suggested earlier, some actual hints as to how or what or something. That's why I was imagining a techno-seance and a commsat. I also would have made the broadcasts less goofy and more sad (dropping the wannabe Galactus thing entirely). What do ghosts whales sing? Probably pining for lost loves and family.

Also what if the TV shows were hallucinatory as individuals translate the whale's loss into cultural referents they understand?

I have a pretty clear vision as to how I would write this one, maybe if there's another reimagining contest…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, My headcanon was that it's just an anomalous terrestrial Bowhead Whale, and the transmissions are products of delusions of grandeur. I never meant to imply the whale was an actual alien being.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the problem people (including myself) is that while the skip itself is interesting, it needs something more.

What the article suggests/hints at from the radii broadcasts is that these space whales go around destory planets and civilizations. One would assume that this is what happened to the froze space whale in Earth's orbit; It came to devour Earth or destory himan civilization but ended up failing somehow. The problem for me is that we never really know or even get a hint at what happened to the whale that caused it to fail its objective. We learn about space whales in general but never what happened to the actual SCP itself.

I believe that is what people are trying to figure out when reading this article, but the article really does not provide this information and so for some it feels like the article comes up short.

How you would go about adding a bit more backstory on the actual frozen whale is up too you though. The article is fine as is, but it if you want to convince some of the no votes to up vote, I think that is what you need to add. If I am wrong about this, hopefully people will correct me. Hope this helped.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Such as? A couple people have mentioned it should be punched up. Suggestions? Also, any thoughts on how I could tighten up those three tropes?
Sorry to put my brainstorming off on you guys- I'm in the middle of another project, and I'm babysitting my niece for a few days. Busy, busy.
And, of course, also, I bow to the terrifying collective will of my peers…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have no idea how I'd incorporate Krill Theater into the article- but that's an awesomely entertaining concept.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is three tropes loosely connected: space whales+anomalous broadcast+lost episodes. It works, but I do somewhat wish there was more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's weird, and the article mostly just rides on the fact of it being weird, but there's this subtle undercurrent of worry, with all the transmissions having destructive or awe-inducing undertones. This is a great example of using logs to give good crunch to an SCP idea.

All in all, +1
It's weird, it's subtly unnerving, it's space whale!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's an interesting object, but then it doesn't end up doing much other than weird anomalous broadcasts, which are a wee bit overdone. This doesn't bring anything new to the table, but it also isn't completely uninteresting. There's definitely a lot of potential here to improve if you can still bring the content of the logs in a form other than an anomalous broadcast. Maybe if it was orbiting earth and this was, I dunno, a live play performed in its mouth by krill.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting and whacky story, plus I think I detect a Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy reference. Nothing that really jumped out and caught my attention though, so no vote. Sorry I can't be more helpful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I missed that episode. South Park did it already? Jesus, how?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't feeling the whole space whale thing initially because it was so bizarre and seemed too random…but then I got to

No launches involving Bowhead Whales, or of a size capable of transporting an intact Bowhead Whale, are presently known to the Foundation.

and had to upvote. In my opinion, the payoff is definitely worth it and the logs tied everything together. The overall tone of this scip is scientific and somehow chilling, which is something you would expect from an SCP article. Yet, it manages to be delightful, funny, and sarcastic at the same time? I don't even know how you did that. It's a strange concept but you somehow sold me on a space whale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted for space whales! Makes me think of the South Park episode where they send a whale into space to bring it back home. And now it's back to eat you!

I also liked the implication that space whales might just, maybe, really exist and it's here to devour us eventually.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is…chilling, for some odd reason. Is the implication that Balena Stellaris actually exists, or is it meant to be open ended?

+1 either way!

item.title: Re: Whoo!
item.content:encoded: Oof. That's just what I need- karmic comeuppance for arrogance. The universe would probably ding me for decades of the stuff. So. Noted. Hubris beaten down with a big stick.

item.title: Re: Whoo!
item.content:encoded: Be careful what you wish for. My first skip is vastly more successful than the other four.

item.title: Whoo!
item.content:encoded: +10 for my first skip! Oh yeah! I am like unto a god! (Cough), ahem. Uh, yeah. Please don't mind the megalomaniacal ranting….

item.title: Re: Oh?
item.content:encoded:
Waaait. That's it. That's everything it needed.

As I said, I give bad advice, but I did hit the spot there.

Now certainly is a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yessss, the site needs more space whale stuff.

item.title: Re: Ah
item.content:encoded: Upvoted. I enjoy this shit a lot.

item.title: Ah
item.content:encoded: Good point, thanks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
seventeen and a half (17.5)

thirty (30)

You need almost none of these. There is no purpose to them other than to state numbers twice for some reason and pad article length. There's one legitimate usage here (the whole "Once every Synodic Lunar month (29.5 days)" bit), but the rest can go. Just go 17.5 and 30 etc.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
May the space whales bless you for writing a skip about space whales.
Space whales that eat up planets and are star-sized.
Space whaaales
You've also contributed to the notably small amount of cetacean-tagged skips, and may they also bless you for that.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …I feel like I should mention that this started out as a -J. Though now, I may have to insert Space Whale Princess Bawooha into all my future works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know if this is overall a good skip, but I'm inclined to think so, and I upvoted for "Space Whale Princess Bawooha" because holy crap, that's perfect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, man

item.title: Re: Oh?
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the tip, I'll do that

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i fucks with it

the premise is interesting. also damn good tone for a newbie, props.

the transmissions themselves are good. i think the last one is a bit anticlimactic though. there's still some room for punch but what's already there is enough for an +1 in my book so w/e

couple of spelling issues here and there but nothing to go crazy about. good work.

item.title: Re: Oh?
item.content:encoded:
Well, yeah. Thing is, radio signals that appear without reason aren't that weird in this site. Neither are objects that appear in places where they couldn't logically be, heck we had a fossilized car from the prehistory and it wasn't considered something too weird.

You have huge story fuel there, I am actually a bit jealous. What I do suggest you though, is to put that "discovery" section above the transmission logs. They lose some effect if there is something afterwards.

item.title: Re: Oh?
item.content:encoded: …It's a lump of pitted whale carcass in orbit that occasionally beams funky radio shows at the earth. It is very much intended to be WTF. Appreciate the feedback, though

item.title: Re: Oh?
item.content:encoded:
I am not the best advice giver here. And it is just my point of view…

It depends in what feeling you are trying to give. I am supposed to feel sorry about this whale? I don't know how it died and it's sending creepy transmissions. I am supposed to be scared? If that's the case, maybe you should reorganize the transmissions, from the less unsettling to the most. Should I just say "WTF, I don't know even…"? Then I don't know how to help you. I suck at that kind of articles.

Maybe someone more experienced can help you with the details.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like your writing style, just the idea seems……weird at best. No vote. Keep it up though.

item.title: Oh?
item.content:encoded: Lacking something…damn. Any ideas how I can punch it up?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ehh. It is weird and cool and everything, but… I don't know, it lacks something. The payoff doesn't really strikes me. I don't know if I should feel sad for it or scared or unsettled.

However…

No launches involving Bowhead Whales, or of a size capable of transporting an intact Bowhead Whale, are presently known to the Foundation.

This sentence, sir, is pure gold.

item.title: Huh
item.content:encoded: Never read it. I'll have to look it up. Also, I'll fix that other thing. Thanks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"…it is presently known…" should be "it is presently unknown…", I do believe.

No vote yet. However, I am mildly surprised that there's been no audio adaptation of one of John Varley's Eight Worlds stories - set in a Solar system in which humans live on every planet except Earth, since the Invaders came to rescue Earth's cetaceans from the human threat. (Although there is a moment in The Ophiuchi Hotline in which one of the doubles of the protagonist winds up on Earth, where she sees a tribe of human primitives that appear to worship a wooden statue of a dolphin.)

item.title: Ahh!
item.content:encoded: Phew! Thanks.
